hero.subtitle: Every button, headline, and layout decision is made with one goal: turning visitors into customers. Design that looks great and performs commercially.

details.title: What We Do & Why It Matters
details.card1Desc: High bounce rates and visitors who leave without converting. Most websites look fine on the surface but fail commercially — unclear messaging, weak CTAs, and poor mobile experience.
details.card2Desc: Conversion-first website and landing page design combining UX strategy, CRO principles, and on-brand visual execution — built to move visitors toward a specific commercial outcome.
details.card3Desc: A website that works as your best salesperson — qualifying visitors, building trust, guiding decisions, and converting at a measurably higher rate.

subFeatures.title: Every Deliverable,
subFeatures.item1Desc: High-converting pages for paid campaigns, product launches, and service offerings — built around a single, clear action.
subFeatures.item2Desc: User experience and interface design that reduces friction, builds trust, and guides visitors naturally toward conversion.
subFeatures.item3Desc: Data-driven improvements to headlines, layouts, CTAs, and form flows to maximise the percentage of visitors who take action.
subFeatures.item4Desc: Pixel-perfect mobile design ensuring the experience on phones is as compelling as on desktop — where most traffic arrives.
subFeatures.item5Desc: On-page SEO, meta structure, site speed, and Core Web Vitals built in from the start — not bolted on after.
subFeatures.item6Desc: Compress, cache, and optimise every asset so your site loads fast — because every second of delay costs conversions.

caseStudy.title: Real Results for Real Businesses
caseStudy.case1Problem: A dated website with a 78% bounce rate and an average of 0.4 enquiries per week. Visitors couldn't quickly understand the offer or find a reason to get in touch.
caseStudy.case2Problem: Running paid ads to a generic homepage with no dedicated landing pages. CPL was high, conversion rate was low, and the mobile experience was broken.
